why_it_matters: This release publishes end-to-end shared playback header support to the public Capacitor package so host apps can declare reusable auth header groups once and apply them safely across Android and iOS playback flows.
user_impact: Capacitor consumers can call `setup({ headerGroups })`, reference them from `Track.headerGroupId`, rely on per-track override precedence, and validate mixed-token/no-leakage behavior with the demo smoke harness.
upgrade_notes: Upgrade to @ddgutierrezc/legato-capacitor@1.1.0 and @ddgutierrezc/legato-contract@1.1.0, then resync native hosts so Android resolves legato-android-core:1.1.0 and iOS resolves LegatoCore v1.1.0.
highlights: Shared playback headers now work across contract, Capacitor bridge, Android, and iOS runtime paths.
Mixed-token playlists, public-track non-leakage, and per-track override precedence are covered by smoke validation.
Strict end-to-end verification completed before publication.
known_limitations: Shared header groups are immutable setup-time declarations; token refresh and DRM remain outside this release scope.
Effective merged headers stay internal and are not exposed through public snapshots or events.
